titleStmt.title: Letter from the the Acetylene Corporation to the Secretary, Irish Office, 18 May 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: The Acetylene Corporation

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the Acetylene Corporation Ltd. to the secretary at the Irish Office in London. In the letter the Corporation offer their services to remove girders and other debris caused by the Easter Rising via the use of an oxy-acetylene blow pipe.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO RP/1916/8653/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
ON ADMIRALTY & WAR OFFICE LISTS TELEGRAMS CONTRACTORS TO H.M. GOVERNMENT, THE PRINCIPAL RAILWAY COMPANIES, TELEPHONE: 'FLAMMA,VIC,LONDON,' BOROUGH AND COUNTY COUNCILS, CROWN AGENTS FOR THE COLONIES &c,&c. VICTORIA 4830. — THE ACETYLENE CORPORATION OF GREAT BRITAIN, LTD., (WITH WHICH IS ASSOCIATED THE BRITISH AIR GAS COMPANY, LIMITED) Branches: Works:; 166, BUCHANAN ST, 19, ESHER STREET, GLASGOW, WESTMINSTER. 19, CROW ST, Carbide Depots: DUBLIN. AT EVERY IMPORTANT PORT 49, Victoria Street, Westminster. IN THE UNITED KINGDOM Ref:- M 18th May 1916 The Secretary, Irish Office, 36 - 38, Old Queen Street, S.W. Dear Sir, In connection with the ruined buildings in Dublin
we should like to bring to your notice that we have a branch at
19, Crow Street, Dublin and are in a position to cut away any
girders and other debris expeditiously by the oxy-acetylene blow
pipe. We have several skilled men in Dublin and the
necessary plant and we could therefore give immediate attention
to any work of this description which may require to be done. Will you be so kind as to bring this matter to the
notice of the responsible authorities as we are not sure to whom
we should apply in Dublin. Yours faithfully, THE ACETYLENE CORPORATION LTD. ack & request that
application might be made
to the Corporation of Dublin
